div.head: The Ambassador in Greece (MacVeagh) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 166. While the King apparently still hesitates (see my No. 164 of December 19, 8 p.m.Not printed.) to agree to British proposals regarding the regency, negotiations for a cessation of hostilities have made some progress here though as yet with no definite results. “Comrade” Porphyrogennis, the Communist emissary of EAM (see my No. 148 of December 13, 3 p.m.), conferred with Plastiras yesterday and according to reliable information stated that it was the desire of the “Left” that Plastiras should be either Regent or Prime Minister since the Left has faith in him as representing the democratic principles for which it is fighting. When Plastiras replied that he wished to stay out of politics, Porphyrogennis said that EAM would accept Sophoulis as Prime Minister. On the military side he then presented a new set of terms on which ELAS would be willing to evacuate Attica but while none of these would appear impossible from the British point of view and made no mention of any amnesty (see my telegram No. 150 of December 15, noon) they were presented as being dependent on Plastiras guaranteeing the democratic political solution desired and also make no mention of the laying down of arms on the part of the ELAS “civilian reserves” here in the city (see my No. 140 of December 11, 3 p.m.) on which point General Scobie has been insistent. Porphyrogennis was expected to see General Scobie here today for futher discussions but sent word that the fighting renders his coming in too dangerous. This may be true but on the other hand after his talk with Plastiras the latter came out with a statement in the pro-Government paper Hellas condemning, in accordance with his views expressed to me (see my No. 155 of December 18, 1 p.m.Not printed.), “anarchistic elements and mischievous gangsters” for preparing and precipitating civil war “after leading astray a certain number of Greek patriots by deceit or force” and it is possible that Porphyrogennis’ decision not to come in to see Scobie today was caused by his having seen this statement.
